title: Bachelor of Vocation (Multimedia and Animation and Motion Design) in association with Ecole Intuit Lab
content: This course offers beginners a concise overview of the visual arts, from traditional to analytical drawing. Through practical exercises, students will learn about artistic principles, develop technical skills, and explore creativity within various mediums. It includes brief lectures, hands-on projects, and critiques, allowing students to build a portfolio of work. By the end of the course, students will have created a portfolio of work demonstrating their acquired skills and understanding of art fundamentals Pass in class 12th in any stream or higher, with a passion for design and a flair for creativity. 01 Demonstrate Understanding of Art Fundamentals – Apply core principles of visual arts, including line, form, perspective, proportion, and composition. 02 Exhibit Technical Proficiency – Use a range of traditional and analytical drawing techniques effectively across multiple mediums. 03 Express Creativity – Explore and communicate original ideas through visual representation and artistic experimentation. 04 Apply Critical and Reflective Thinking – Evaluate and refine their own work as well as provide constructive feedback on the work of peers. 05 Develop a Professional Portfolio – Present a coherent body of creative work that reflects technical skills, conceptual understanding, and artistic growth 06 Pursue Further Learning Opportunities – Build a strong foundation for advanced study or specialization in art, design, and related creative fields. 01 Build a Strong Foundation – Provide students with fundamental knowledge of visual arts, including artistic principles, techniques, and creative processes. 02 Enhance Technical and Creative Skills – Develop students’ ability to apply drawing and design techniques effectively while encouraging imaginative and original expression. 03 Cultivate Critical and Aesthetic Understanding – Foster the ability to observe, analyze, and appreciate art in cultural, historical, and contemporary contexts. 04 Encourage Lifelong Learning and Growth – Inspire students to pursue advanced studies, professional opportunities, and self-directed learning in the field of art and design. 05 Prepare for Academic and Career Pathways – Enable students to build a portfolio that reflects their artistic abilities and supports progression into higher education or creative careers.
